hooks.name: do_graphql_request
hooks.description: Legacy GraphQL request lifecycle action retained for backward compatibility.

hooks.name: enqueue_graphiql_extension
hooks.description: Legacy GraphiQL extension enqueue action retained for backward compatibility.

hooks.name: graphql_admin_notices_render_notice
hooks.description: Fires for each admin notice that is rendered.

params.name: $notice_slug
params.description: The slug of the notice

params.name: $notice
params.description: The notice to be rendered

params.name: $is_dismissable
params.description: Whether the notice is dismissable or not

params.name: $count
params.description: The count of the notice

hooks.name: graphql_admin_notices_render_notices
hooks.description: Fires before the admin notices are rendered.

params.name: $notices
params.description: The notices to be rendered

hooks.name: graphql_after_execute
hooks.description: Run an action after GraphQL Execution

params.name: $filtered_response
params.description: The response of the entire operation. Could be a single operation or a batch operation

params.name: $request
params.description: Instance of the Request being executed

hooks.name: graphql_after_resolve_field
hooks.description: Fire an action AFTER the field resolves

params.name: $source
params.description: The source passed down the Resolve Tree

params.name: $args
params.description: The args for the field

params.name: $context
params.description: The AppContext passed down the ResolveTree

params.name: $info
params.description: The ResolveInfo passed down the ResolveTree

params.name: $field_resolver
params.description: The Resolve function for the field

params.name: $type_name
params.description: The name of the type the fields belong to

params.name: $field_key
params.description: The name of the field

params.name: $field
params.description: The Field Definition for the resolving field

params.name: $result
params.description: The result of the field resolver

hooks.name: graphql_after_return_field_from_model
hooks.description: Hook that fires after the data is returned for the field

params.name: $result
params.description: The returned data for the field

params.name: $key
params.description: The name of the field on the type

params.name: $model_name
params.description: Name of the model the filter is currently being executed in

params.name: $data
params.description: The un-modeled incoming data

params.name: $visibility
params.description: The visibility setting for this piece of data

params.name: $owner
params.description: The user ID for the owner of this piece of data

params.name: $current_user
params.description: The current user for the session

hooks.name: graphql_before_execute
hooks.description: This action runs before execution of a GraphQL request (regardless if it's a single or batch request)

params.name: $request
params.description: The instance of the Request being executed

hooks.name: graphql_before_resolve_field
hooks.description: Fire an action BEFORE the field resolves

params.name: $source
params.description: The source passed down the Resolve Tree

params.name: $args
params.description: The args for the field

params.name: $context
params.description: The AppContext passed down the ResolveTree

params.name: $info
params.description: The ResolveInfo passed down the ResolveTree

params.name: $field_resolver
params.description: The Resolve function for the field

params.name: $type_name
params.description: The name of the type the fields belong to

params.name: $field_key
params.description: The name of the field

params.name: $field
params.description: The Field Definition for the resolving field

hooks.name: graphql_connection
hooks.description: Legacy connection result filter retained for backward compatibility.

params.name: $query_analyzer
params.description: The instance of the query analyzer

params.name: $query
params.description: The query string being executed

hooks.name: graphql_do_request
hooks.description: Run an action for each request.

params.name: $query
params.description: The GraphQL query

params.name: $operation
params.description: The name of the operation

params.name: $variables
params.description: Variables to be passed to your GraphQL request

params.name: $params
params.description: The Operation Params. This includes any extra params,

hooks.name: graphql_do_update_routine
hooks.description: Fires the update routine.

params.name: $stored_version
params.description: The version number currently stored in the database.

params.name: $new_version
params.description: The version number of the current plugin.

hooks.name: graphql_endpoint
hooks.description: Legacy endpoint path filter retained for backward compatibility.

hooks.name: graphql_endpoint
hooks.description: Legacy endpoint path filter retained for backward compatibility.

hooks.name: graphql_enqueue_graphiql_extension
hooks.description: Fires before GraphiQL extensions are enqueued.

hooks.name: graphql_execute
hooks.description: Legacy GraphQL request execution action retained for backward compatibility.

hooks.name: graphql_execute_batch_queries
hooks.description: Execute batch queries

params.name: $params
params.description: The operation params of the batch request

hooks.name: graphql_experiments_loaded
hooks.description: Fires after all the experiments have been loaded.

params.name: $experiments
params.description: The list of loaded experiment classes, keyed by experiment slug.

params.name: $registry
params.description: The list of registered experiment classes, keyed by experiment slug.

hooks.name: graphql_experiments_registered
hooks.description: Fires after the experiment classes have been registered.

params.name: $registry
params.description: The list of registered experiment classes, keyed by experiment slug.

hooks.name: graphql_get_debug_log
hooks.description: Init the debug logger

params.name: $instance
params.description: The DebugLog instance

hooks.name: graphql_get_schema
hooks.description: Fire an action when the Schema is returned

hooks.name: graphql_get_type_registry
hooks.description: Fire an action when the Type Registry is returned

hooks.name: graphql_init
hooks.description: Fire off init action

params.name: $instance
params.description: The instance of the WPGraphQL class

hooks.name: graphql_init_request
hooks.description: Fires when initializing a GraphQL request from WP-CLI.

hooks.name: graphql_init_request
hooks.description: Action – intentionally with no context – to indicate a GraphQL Request has started.

hooks.name: graphql_init_type_registry
hooks.description: Fire an action as the Type registry is being initiated.

params.name: $registry
params.description: Instance of the TypeRegistry.

hooks.name: graphql_insert_{$post_type_object->name}
hooks.description: Fires after a single term is created or updated via a GraphQL mutation The dynamic portion of the hook name, `$taxonomy->name` refers to the taxonomy of the term being mutated

params.name: $post_id
params.description: Inserted post ID

params.name: $args
params.description: The args used to insert the term

params.name: $mutation_name
params.description: The name of the mutation being performed

hooks.name: graphql_insert_{$taxonomy->name}
hooks.description: Fires after a single term is created or updated via a GraphQL mutation The dynamic portion of the hook name, `$taxonomy->name` refers to the taxonomy of the term being mutated

params.name: $term_id
params.description: Inserted term object

params.name: $args
params.description: The args used to insert the term

params.name: $mutation_name
params.description: The name of the mutation being performed

params.name: $context
params.description: The AppContext passed down the resolve tree

params.name: $info
params.description: The ResolveInfo passed down the resolve tree

hooks.name: graphql_insert_post_object
hooks.description: Fires after a single term is created or updated via a GraphQL mutation The dynamic portion of the hook name, `$taxonomy->name` refers to the taxonomy of the term being mutated

params.name: $post_id
params.description: Inserted post ID

params.name: $post_type_object
params.description: The Post Type object for the post being mutated

params.name: $args
params.description: The args used to insert the term

params.name: $mutation_name
params.description: The name of the mutation being performed

hooks.name: graphql_insert_term
hooks.description: Fires after a single term is created or updated via a GraphQL mutation

params.name: $term_id
params.description: Inserted term object

params.name: $taxonomy
params.description: The taxonomy of the term being updated

params.name: $args
params.description: The args used to insert the term

params.name: $mutation_name
params.description: The name of the mutation being performed

params.name: $context
params.description: The AppContext passed down the resolve tree

params.name: $info
params.description: The ResolveInfo passed down the resolve tree

hooks.name: graphql_media_item_mutation_update_additional_data
hooks.description: Run an action after the additional data has been updated. This is a great spot to hook into to update additional data related to mediaItems, such as updating additional postmeta, or sending emails to Kevin. . .whatever you need to do with the mediaItem.

params.name: $media_item_id
params.description: The ID of the mediaItem being mutated

params.name: $input
params.description: The input for the mutation

params.name: $post_type_object
params.description: The Post Type Object for the type of post being mutated

params.name: $mutation_name
params.description: The name of the mutation (ex: create, update, delete)

params.name: $context
params.description: The AppContext that is passed down the resolve tree

params.name: $info
params.description: The ResolveInfo that is passed down the resolve tree

hooks.name: graphql_mutation_response
hooks.description: Fires after the mutation payload has been returned from the `mutateAndGetPayload` callback.

params.name: $payload
params.description: The Payload returned from the mutation.

params.name: $input
params.description: The mutation input args, after being filtered by 'graphql_mutation_input'.

params.name: $unfiltered_input
params.description: The unfiltered input args of the mutation

params.name: $context
params.description: The AppContext object.

params.name: $info
params.description: The ResolveInfo object.

params.name: $mutation_name
params.description: The name of the mutation field.

hooks.name: graphql_post_object_mutation_set_object_terms
hooks.description: Fire an action before setting object terms during a GraphQL Post Object Mutation. One example use for this hook would be to create terms from the input that may not exist yet, so that they can be set as a relation below.

params.name: $post_id
params.description: The ID of the postObject being mutated

params.name: $input
params.description: The input for the mutation

params.name: $post_type_object
params.description: The Post Type Object for the type of post being mutated

params.name: $mutation_name
params.description: The name of the mutation (ex: create, update, delete)

hooks.name: graphql_post_object_mutation_update_additional_data
hooks.description: Run an action after the additional data has been updated. This is a great spot to hook into to update additional data related to postObjects, such as setting relationships, updating additional postmeta, or sending emails to Kevin. . .whatever you need to do with the postObject.

params.name: $post_id
params.description: The ID of the postObject being mutated

params.name: $input
params.description: The input for the mutation

params.name: $post_type_object
params.description: The Post Type Object for the type of post being mutated

params.name: $mutation_name
params.description: The name of the mutation (ex: create, update, delete)

params.name: $context
params.description: The AppContext passed down to all resolvers

params.name: $info
params.description: The ResolveInfo passed down to all resolvers

params.name: $intended_post_status
params.description: The intended post_status the post should have according to the mutation input

params.name: $default_post_status
params.description: The default status posts should use if an intended status wasn't set

hooks.name: graphql_process_http_request
hooks.description: This action can be hooked to to enable various debug tools, such as enableValidation from the GraphQL Config.

hooks.name: graphql_process_http_request_response
hooks.description: Run an action after the HTTP Response is ready to be sent back. This might be a good place for tools to hook in to track metrics, such as how long the process took from `graphql_process_http_request` to here, etc.

params.name: $response
params.description: The GraphQL response

params.name: $result
params.description: Deprecated. Same as $response.

params.name: $operation_name
params.description: The name of the operation

params.name: $query
params.description: The request that GraphQL executed

params.name: $variables
params.description: Variables to passed to your GraphQL query

params.name: $status_code
params.description: The status code for the response

hooks.name: graphql_register_initial_types
hooks.description: Fire an action as the type registry is initialized. This executes before the `graphql_register_types` action to allow for earlier hooking

params.name: $registry
params.description: Instance of the TypeRegistry

hooks.name: graphql_register_types
hooks.description: Fire an action as the type registry is initialized. This executes before the `graphql_register_types` action to allow for earlier hooking

params.name: $registry
params.description: Instance of the TypeRegistry

hooks.name: graphql_register_types_late
hooks.description: Fire an action as the type registry is initialized. This executes during the `graphql_register_types` action to allow for earlier hooking

params.name: $registry
params.description: Instance of the TypeRegistry

hooks.name: graphql_request_execute
hooks.description: Run an action. This is a good place for debug tools to hook in to log things, etc.

params.name: $response
params.description: The response your GraphQL request

params.name: $schema
params.description: The schema object for the root request

params.name: $operation
params.description: The name of the operation

params.name: $query
params.description: The query that GraphQL executed

params.name: $variables
params.description: Variables to passed to your GraphQL query

params.name: $request
params.description: Instance of the Request

hooks.name: graphql_response_set_headers
hooks.description: Fire an action when the headers are set

params.name: $headers
params.description: The headers sent in the response

hooks.name: graphql_return_response
hooks.description: Run an action after the response has been filtered, as the response is being returned. This is a good place for debug tools to hook in to log things, etc.

params.name: $filtered_response
params.description: The filtered response for the GraphQL request

params.name: $response
params.description: The response for your GraphQL request

params.name: $schema
params.description: The schema object for the root request

params.name: $operation
params.description: The name of the operation

params.name: $query
params.description: The query that GraphQL executed

params.name: $variables
params.description: Variables to passed to your GraphQL query

params.name: $request
params.description: Instance of the Request

params.name: $query_id
params.description: The query id that GraphQL executed

hooks.name: graphql_server_config
hooks.description: Run an action when the server config is created. The config can be acted upon directly to override default values or implement new features, e.g., $config->setValidationRules().

params.name: $config
params.description: Server config

params.name: $params
params.description: Request operation params

hooks.name: graphql_update_{$taxonomy->name}
hooks.description: Fires an action when a term is updated via a GraphQL Mutation

params.name: $term_id
params.description: The ID of the term object that was mutated

params.name: $args
params.description: The args used to update the term

params.name: $mutation_name
params.description: The name of the mutation being performed (create, update, delete, etc)

params.name: $context
params.description: The AppContext passed down the resolve tree

params.name: $info
params.description: The ResolveInfo passed down the resolve tree

hooks.name: graphql_update_term
hooks.description: Fires an action when a term is updated via a GraphQL Mutation

params.name: $term_id
params.description: The ID of the term object that was mutated

params.name: $taxonomy
params.description: The taxonomy of the term being updated

params.name: $args
params.description: The args used to update the term

params.name: $mutation_name
params.description: The name of the mutation being performed (create, update, delete, etc)

params.name: $context
params.description: The AppContext passed down the resolve tree

params.name: $info
params.description: The ResolveInfo passed down the resolve tree

hooks.name: graphql_user_object_mutation_update_additional_data
hooks.description: Run an action after the additional data has been updated. This is a great spot to hook into to update additional data related to users, such as setting relationships, updating additional usermeta, or sending emails to Kevin... whatever you need to do with the userObject.

params.name: $user_id
params.description: The ID of the user being mutated

params.name: $input
params.description: The input for the mutation

params.name: $mutation_name
params.description: The name of the mutation (ex: create, update, delete)

params.name: $context
params.description: The AppContext passed down the resolve tree

params.name: $info
params.description: The ResolveInfo passed down the Resolve Tree
